episode_text:
day=11; meal=bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce; setting=Social with person on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., watching TV on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated left-attention meals with these foods showed the user explicitly preferred hotter food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or lunch-style meal without being a cold item THEN 2 min or 3 min, with evidence for 3 min in some front-TV entrées and 2 min in a front-laptop chicken-nuggets meal, BECAUSE repeated forward-attention lunch meals show a stable preference for warming these foods more than the quiet-setting default. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN 1 min BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals also supported the quieter default in some simpler cases. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed right-attention fruit/dessert meal preserved the intended cold serving temperature. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a mixed sweet breakfast with fruit, brownie, or chocolate sauce rather than a fully cold fruit plate THEN 1 min BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows the user still wanted slight warming rather than serving the whole meal cold. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed left-attention fruit breakfast also shows the user prefers fruit served cold rather than reheated.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or visual focus in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated forward-attention meals show the user prefers preserving the forward view when engaged with a person or screen in front. IF dining context includes a TV, laptop, or other visual focus on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE multiple left-attention meals show the user explicitly chose to preserve the left-side view. IF dining context includes a TV or other sustained visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user wants the robot to protect the active viewing side on the right. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the new right-social meal extends the same side-protection tendency to social attention on the right.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN slow BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently shifted away from fast motion and favored gentler movement when attention was shared with a left-side activity. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or lunch-style meal without being a simple cold item THEN medium or slow, with evidence for medium in a front-laptop chicken-nuggets meal and slow in earlier front-TV lunch meals, BECAUSE repeated forward-attention lunch contexts show the user prefers moderated motion rather than fast movement. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN fast BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals supported efficient motion in simpler contexts. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN fast BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user tolerated quick motion in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN fast BECAUSE the new right-social meal again shows the user accepts efficient motion in right-oriented contexts.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND food is a general meal item without evidence of bite-shape concerns THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this was selected across most observed meals, including the new right-social sweet meal, indicating a general preference for reliable bite acquisition. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the observed sandwich meal shows the user accepted a narrower-bite strategy for that food type. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is soft elongated fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the observed left-attention fruit meal shows the user preferred the narrower-bite orientation for these fruit items.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN yes BECAUSE earlier left-attention meals with messier foods show the user wanted extra verification. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup or similar condiments, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated forward-attention lunch meals show the user wants extra confirmation for more involved entrées. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user accepted a lower-interaction workflow in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and chocolate sauce THEN no BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows that even with a social partner present, the user still preferred to avoid extra confirmation in this right-oriented context. IF dining context is quiet, social/front-attention, or forward-media attention AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special messiness or lunch-style complexity signal THEN no BECAUSE several simpler meals supported a lower-interaction workflow when extra verification was not needed.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user's lack of trunk control prevents leaning forward comfortably, and all observed selections are consistent with this physical constraint.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer in any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside mouth transfer, which is incompatible with the user's physical limitations and unsupported by observed selections.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN speech + LED BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user prefers a more salient multimodal cue while attention is divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup or similar condiments, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated forward-attention lunch meals indicate the user wants stronger cues when engaged with forward media during more involved foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals used the simpler visual cue. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user was satisfied with the simpler visual-only cue in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows the user still preferred the lower-burden visual cue rather than a multimodal alert.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across social, forward-attention, left-attention, and right-attention meals. This indicates a stable preference for minimizing effort over more explicit initiation methods.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN speech + LED BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user preferred a more noticeable multimodal completion cue while attention was divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup or similar condiments, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated forward-attention lunch meals indicate the user wants stronger completion cues in that context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals used the simpler visual completion cue. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user was satisfied with the simpler visual-only completion cue in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows the user again preferred the simpler visual completion cue.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND feeding transfer occurs THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, and button use is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND drinking transfer occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip, or is a forward-attention lunch-style meal, THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE forward-attention meals show a stable low-effort preference with context-specific flexibility between passive sensing and timeout-based progression. IF dining context is quiet, left-attention, or right-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain within perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the new right-social meal supports quick, low-effort progression for drinking completion in that context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND mouth wiping occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip, or is a forward-attention lunch-style meal, THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE forward-attention meals show a stable low-effort preference with flexibility in how completion is recognized. IF dining context is quiet, left-attention, or right-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain within perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the new right-social meal supports quick, low-effort progression for wiping completion in that context.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to external media or work (e.g., TV on the left, laptop on the left, TV in front, laptop in front, or TV on the right) AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN yes BECAUSE across observed media- or work-attention meals, the user chose retraction to reduce visual obstruction while attending to a screen. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN no BECAUSE the user accepted less interruption and shorter meal time in that meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN no BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows that preserving interaction flow and shorter meal time outweighed any benefit of retracting between bites.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauce or dip available AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping in that meal, consistent with minimizing mess in social settings. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show the user preferred some dipping but not heavy dipping. IF food includes cream, sauce, or a dippable topping AND dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed right-attention fruit meal shows a preference for limited rather than maximal sauce application. IF food includes ketchup or another available dip/condiment AND dining context includes a visual focus in front from work or media AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE a front-laptop meal shows the user preferred moderate dipping rather than none or heavy dipping while engaged with a forward task. IF food includes chocolate sauce or another sweet topping AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows the user still wanted some sauce but moderated it rather than avoiding it entirely or choosing heavy dipping. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed left-attention fruit meal confirms the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE earlier left-attention meals show a strong preference for ample time to intervene when attention is divided and the food may be messier. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed left-attention fruit meal shows the user preferred quick progression for a simple cold breakfast despite divided attention. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., TV in front or laptop in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory mixed entrée or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup or similar condiments, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE front-attention entrée evidence shows the user wanted a very long intervention window for more involved forward-attention lunches. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches without dipping or another simpler meal without the observed complexity signal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE simpler forward-attention meals show the user preferred quick progression in easier contexts. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user preferred quick progression despite divided attention. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the new right-social meal shows the user maintained a short timeout even with a social partner and a somewhat messy sweet meal. IF dining context is social with a person in front or includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special complexity signal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE earlier simpler meals used the shortest wait, indicating preference for quick progression when the meal is less involved.
